library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.6
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.1     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.0     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(janitor)
## 
## Attaching package: 'janitor'

## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

##     chisq.test, fisher.test
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggplot2)
library(paletteer)
library(ggmap)
## ℹ Google's Terms of Service: <https://mapsplatform.google.com>
##   Stadia Maps' Terms of Service: <https://stadiamaps.com/terms-of-service>

## ℹ Please cite ggmap if you use it! Use `citation("ggmap")` for details.
library(patchwork)
library(ggdensity)
library(paletteer)
library(RColorBrewer)
setwd("~/Desktop/BIS15L_W2026_Group-8/rbird")

Data

sample_data <- read_csv("sample_data.csv") %>%
  clean_names()
## Rows: 62298 Columns: 12
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (6): locality, observer_id, sampling_event_identifier, observation_type,...
## dbl (6): latitude, longitude, duration_minutes, effort_distance_km, number_o...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
species_data <- read_csv("species_data.csv") %>%
  clean_names()
## Rows: 685539 Columns: 16
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (8): common_name, scientific_name, locality, month, day, observer_id, sa...
## dbl (8): taxonomic_order, observation_count, latitude, longitude, year, dura...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
total_data <- read_csv("total_data.csv") %>%
  clean_names()
## Rows: 704373 Columns: 15
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (8): common_name, scientific_name, locality.y, observer_id.y, sampling_e...
## dbl (7): observation_count, latitude.y, longitude.y, duration_minutes.y, eff...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Removing data from total_data where the only information is sampling_event_identifier

#total_data <- total_data %>%
#  filter(!is.na(common_name))

How many bird species are represented by the data?

total_data %>% 
  distinct(common_name) %>% 
  count()
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##       n
##   <int>
## 1   395

Top five bird observations between 2019 and 2021 (total)

top_five_total <- total_data %>%
  filter(year_y %in% c(2019, 2020, 2021)) %>% 
  group_by(common_name) %>%
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(count)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
top_five_total
## # A tibble: 5 × 2
##   common_name           count
##   <chr>                 <int>
## 1 Black Phoebe          17705
## 2 House Finch           14295
## 3 Mourning Dove         13683
## 4 California Scrub-Jay  13598
## 5 White-crowned Sparrow 12009
top_five_total %>% 
ggplot(aes(x = common_name, y = count, fill = common_name)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip()+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2")+
  labs(title = "Top Five Birds with Most Observations",
       x = "Common Name",
       y = "Count of Observations",
       fill="Common Name") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.position = "none")+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = top_five_total$common_name)

Top five birds with most observations per each year

top_five <- total_data %>%
  filter(year_y %in% c(2019, 2020, 2021)) %>% 
  group_by(year_y, common_name) %>%
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(count)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'year_y'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
top_five
## # A tibble: 15 × 3
## # Groups:   year_y [3]
##    year_y common_name           count
##     <dbl> <chr>                 <int>
##  1   2019 Black Phoebe           6040
##  2   2019 Mourning Dove          4594
##  3   2019 House Finch            4532
##  4   2019 California Scrub-Jay   4483
##  5   2019 Mallard                4060
##  6   2020 Black Phoebe           5798
##  7   2020 House Finch            4842
##  8   2020 Mourning Dove          4476
##  9   2020 California Scrub-Jay   4451
## 10   2020 White-crowned Sparrow  4139
## 11   2021 Black Phoebe           5867
## 12   2021 House Finch            4921
## 13   2021 California Scrub-Jay   4664
## 14   2021 Mourning Dove          4613
## 15   2021 White-crowned Sparrow  4136

Yellow-rumped Warbler 2019 (to readjust battle)

total_data %>% 
  filter(year_y=="2019", common_name=="Yellow-rumped Warbler") %>%
  count()
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##       n
##   <int>
## 1  3943

White-crowned Sparrow 2019 & 2019+2020 (to readjust battle)

total_data %>% 
  filter(year_y=="2019", common_name=="White-crowned Sparrow") %>%
  count()
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##       n
##   <int>
## 1  3734
total_data %>% 
  filter(year_y %in% c(2019, 2020), common_name=="White-crowned Sparrow") %>%
  count()
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##       n
##   <int>
## 1  7873

Finding totals as years stack

top_observations_19 <- total_data %>% 
  filter(year_y=="2019") %>% 
  group_by(common_name) %>% 
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(count)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
top_observations_19
## # A tibble: 5 × 2
##   common_name          count
##   <chr>                <int>
## 1 Black Phoebe          6040
## 2 Mourning Dove         4594
## 3 House Finch           4532
## 4 California Scrub-Jay  4483
## 5 Mallard               4060
top_observations_19 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = common_name, y = count, fill = common_name)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip()+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2")+
  labs(title = "Top Bird Observations 2019",
       x = "Common Name",
       y = "Count of Observations",
       fill="Common Name") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.position = "none")+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = top_observations_19$common_name)

top_observations_19_20 <- total_data %>% 
  filter(year_y %in% c(2019, 2020)) %>% 
  group_by(common_name) %>% 
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(count)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
top_observations_19_20
## # A tibble: 5 × 2
##   common_name           count
##   <chr>                 <int>
## 1 Black Phoebe          11838
## 2 House Finch            9374
## 3 Mourning Dove          9070
## 4 California Scrub-Jay   8934
## 5 Yellow-rumped Warbler  7911
top_observations_19_20 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = common_name, y = count, fill = common_name)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip()+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2")+
  labs(title = "Top Bird Observations 2019 & 2020",
       x = "Common Name",
       y = "Count of Observations",
       fill="Common Name") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.position = "none")+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = top_observations_19_20$common_name)

top_observations_19_20_21 <- total_data %>% 
  filter(year_y %in% c(2019, 2020, 2021)) %>% 
  group_by(common_name) %>% 
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(count)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
top_observations_19_20_21
## # A tibble: 5 × 2
##   common_name           count
##   <chr>                 <int>
## 1 Black Phoebe          17705
## 2 House Finch           14295
## 3 Mourning Dove         13683
## 4 California Scrub-Jay  13598
## 5 White-crowned Sparrow 12009
top_observations_19_20_21 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = common_name, y = count, fill = common_name)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip()+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2")+
  labs(title = "Top Bird Observations 2019, 2020, & 2021 Combined",
       x = "Common Name",
       y = "Count of Observations",
       fill="Common Name") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.position = "none")+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = top_observations_19_20_21$common_name)

Top 5 observations in separate years organized from least to most

top_five %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year_y, y = count, fill = common_name)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  coord_flip()+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2")+
  labs(title = "Top Five Birds with Most Observations",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Count of Observations",
       fill="Common Name") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14))

Shiny Questions -

What is your favourite season? (Winter, Spring, Summer, Fall) Do you prefer when people go the extra mile for you, would you rather meet them halfway, or do you like when others do all the work? (effort_distance_km_y >= 1, effort_distance_km<= 1, effort_distance_km == NA (or 0)) Do you prefer to be around a lot of people or do you like to keep to yourself? (number_observers_y >= 5) Where is your favourite place to hang out in Davis? (different hotspots - not sure this will work though, need distinctly varying birds per hotspot)
